<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> A word that offers forgiveness, a word that tells stories that are backed up by actions that reveal a profound trust in God who is so close to us in Christ, as close as our own heartbeats.<|16.88|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> What this word does is invite us and gives us the power to feel this word and ultimately turn toward its source.<|13.50|>
<|14.14|> Turn toward where it comes from, which is Almighty God, the maker of all things and all people.<|22.36|>
<|22.50|> A word that comes from God and resonates with what we hear within our own selves.<|29.40|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|2.08|> Christmas carols are words with feeling, of course, and it's really my favorite thing to do on Christmas Eve is sing Christmas carols.<|11.08|>
<|11.16|> And I've been thinking a lot about what is my favorite Christmas carol, I think, for 2020, which is A Little Town of Bethlehem, written by a minister.<|20.96|>
<|21.26|> Of all things, a minister wrote this. I do not have that gift.<|25.24|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And I love this hymn, this carol, because of one line which is so unsentimental, especially when compared to other Christmas carols.<|10.14|>
<|11.20|> The hopes and fears of all the years are met in thee tonight.<|18.40|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It's the perfect carol, unfortunately, for 2020,<|4.46|>
<|4.80|> a year in which we have felt a lot of fear and a lot of longing,<|10.32|>
<|11.12|> perhaps even a lot of hope for what we do not yet possess.<|14.28|>
<|15.06|> In just a moment, the choir will sing it.<|17.34|>
<|17.94|> And even if you can't carry a tune, sing along with us.<|21.76|>
<|21.86|> Turn your volume up if it helps.<|23.62|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The hopes and fears of all the years are met in thee tonight.<|6.38|>
<|6.50|> It takes a great deal of courage and self-awareness to name what it is that makes you afraid.<|15.68|>
<|17.44|> And what so many of us are afraid of is that we are alone.<|21.94|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> moon.<|1.26|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.80|> And the promise of Christmas and the sign that is Jesus Christ is God's promise and sign that we<|9.04|>
<|9.04|> are not alone. This word of God reverberates, reverberates and resonates within our hearts<|17.38|>
<|17.38|> if we could but listen and open our ears to hear.<|20.80|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It also takes a great deal of courage and self-awareness to name, if just to ourselves, what we hope for most, what we long for.<|13.04|>
<|13.18|> And for so many people in this world, hope is desperate and urgent.<|18.30|>
<|18.64|> Hope for security, peace, a roof over one's head, food and friendship.<|27.08|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And the sign and promise of Christmas that is Jesus Christ is that God, almighty God, the maker of all things and all people, longs and is actively working to give everyone and every culture and every family what they need.<|18.90|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.90|> It's a deep and unsentimental line.<|3.28|>
<|3.56|> The hopes and fears of all the years are met in thee tonight.<|7.82|>
<|7.94|> But when we sing it and put our hearts into it,<|10.68|>
<|11.36|> it's a line full of feeling and even faith.<|16.00|>
